theme: Keys of Releasing the Miracle Working Power of the Holy Ghost
summary: On Pentecost the Holy Spirit came with all the anointing power for the church. This teaching explores spiritual keys that fan the power of the Spirit into flame so miracle working power can be released.
fullNotes: On Pentecost the Holy Spirit came with all the anointing power for the church. However, the power of the Holy Spirit is not automatic for the believer. For the Holy Spirit to release miracle working power, He has to be fanned into flame. To do this, we need to deploy spiritual keys to fan the power of the Spirit for the miraculous to happen.

sessions.title: Key #1: Be in One Accord in Prayer
sessions.comment: It is absolutely important that believers gather together in unity to fellowship and pray. Power is only given to those in unity of fellowship. No unity, no love, no power. You cannot walk in power if you are a lone ranger. Stay-at-home believers cannot exercise spiritual power. Being too busy to be with the church is a sign of no power.

sessions.title: Key #2: Laying on of Hands
sessions.comment: The frequent laying on of hands by mature ministers on those in regular fellowship and unity with the church grants access to the power of the Holy Spirit.

sessions.title: Key #3: Prophetic Praise, Prophetic Tongues & Prophesy
sessions.comment: The Holy Spirit loves an atmosphere of prophetic praise and worship. In this atmosphere, praise God with all kinds of tongues. Praise God with your native tongue and other unknown tongues. Start with tongues you know and understand and grow. Prophetic worship and tongues exalt God on what He has done. After the tongues, begin to prophesy. To prophesy is to give assignment to the Holy Spirit on what to do. Speak, declare and decree things in the atmosphere of worship.

sessions.title: Key #4: Use of Prophetic Materials
sessions.comment: Material things can be used to arouse the power of the Holy Spirit to act. Things in nature have ears and voices; they speak to the spiritual world. When subjected to God, they are used by the Holy Spirit as signs to activate heavenly agenda. You need to discern which material to use, when, how and where. Never use these materials prophetically if you are not fully immersed in Christ.

author: Elder Yaakov

theme: Faith Actions That Activate Heavenly Response After Prayer
summary: Prayer authorizes heaven to act on earth through the Holy Spirit and holy angels. After prayer, believers must also take actions of faith so heaven can respond to the situation needing mercy.
fullNotes: When you pray, angels collect the prayers from the Holy Spirit and offer them to the four living creatures and the twenty four elders who sit around the throne of God. In turn, the four living creatures and the twenty four elders offer the prayers of the saints to the Father and Jesus Christ. After these offerings of prayer, the Holy Spirit and the angels responsible for human affairs are authorized to take action. The hand of God on earth is activated through the Holy Spirit and His holy angels.

If we do not pray, the Holy Spirit and angels are idle, the four living creatures and the twenty four elders have nothing to offer at the throne of God. See Revelation 5:8-10 and Revelation 8:3-5. Without prayer, heaven cannot help earth. Human prayer is the energy and license that authorizes heaven to move on earth.

However, for the Holy Spirit and angels to produce answers and miracles on earth, there are faith actions that those who pray must perform after prayer, for heaven to act. That is to say that for heaven to act, we need prayer plus actions of faith. Faith without action is dead. Prayer without faith actions is dead.

It is important to understand that prayer is directed to heaven to authorize heaven to act on a situation. But a faith action is directed at the object needing mercy by another human being, so that heaven can act upon that situation. After prayer, heaven always waits for a human action of faith before it can respond. Many prayers are never answered because we are afraid to carry out actions of faith after prayer.

You must discern each situation and get revelation on what faith action is needed in what situation. If one is full of prayer and fasting, the Holy Spirit will reveal what faith action is needed. Remember, anyone who is full of prayer and fasting can perform faith actions. It is not for ministers alone, but for the saints.

sessions.title: Faith Action #1: Bless, Lay Hands, Anoint With Oil
sessions.comment: To produce miracle power from the Holy Spirit and angels, it is important to move by actions of faith and bless, lay hands and anoint with oil the person or object upon which we need heaven to act after prayer. These can be done for people, animals, land, trees and objects. Bless, lay hands, and anoint with oil in the name of Jesus through the power of the Holy Spirit.

sessions.title: Faith Action #2: Command, Cast Out & Rebuke Evil Spirits & Demons
sessions.comment: The second faith action is by commanding demons and evil spirits to be cast out from people, animals, places, objects and other situations in the name of Jesus by the power of the Holy Spirit. When engaging demons and wicked spirits, one must be full of prayer and fasting.

sessions.title: Faith Action #3: Prophesy, Decree, Declare & Do Prophetic Actions
sessions.comment: The third faith action is to prophesy, do prophetic actions, decree and declare logos and rhema revelations that the Holy Spirit puts in your heart and mouth. The best moments to prophesy and decree prophetic declarations and actions is after an intense prayer, praise and worship session. Spiritual songs and hymns arouse the prophetic grace. Prophesy boldly. Speak prophetically to the person or thing needing the prophetic power of the Holy Spirit. Some things will look unusual when you act prophetically.

sessions.title: Pentecost
sessions.comment: Special Pentecost Bible Study focus.

sessions.title: Pentecost
sessions.comment: Special Pentecost prayer and reflection.

summary: Prayer that activates the power of the Holy Spirit, the name of Jesus, and angelic armies has four stages: worship, thanksgiving, confession, and petitions.
fullNotes: Prayer that activates the power of the Holy Spirit, activates the power of the name of Jesus, and causes God to send His angelic armies has four main stages. To pray powerfully and fervently in the Holy Spirit, it is important to pray accordingly at each stage.
Let us study these important prayer stages. When you pray, plan time to attend to all four stages, giving each stage enough time.

sessions.title: Prayer Stage 1: Worship the Father & Jesus Christ - Light the Fire of the Holy Ghost
sessions.comment: The first stage when you approach the throne of the Father and of Jesus Christ is worship. Worship can be in spoken words, chants or singing, in known and unknown tongues. Worship acknowledges who the Father is and who Jesus Christ is. It is adoration. The only heavenly beings to be given worship are the Father and Jesus Christ. Worship of anything else is idolatry. First you worship the Father and then you worship Jesus Christ. Worship lights fire to prayer and attracts God's attention.

sessions.title: Prayer Stage 2: Thanksgiving - Fan the Fire of the Holy Ghost
sessions.comment: The second stage of prayer is to give thanks to the Father and to Jesus Christ. Thanksgiving fans prayer into a powerful flame. It recognizes and appreciates what the Father, through Christ and the power of the Holy Spirit, has already done, is doing, and will do according to His will. Thanksgiving creates bonding, deep fellowship, oneness, and close friendship with God.

sessions.title: Prayer Stage 3: Confession - Activate the Blood of Jesus
sessions.comment: The third stage of prayer is confession. Confession gives power to the blood of Jesus to speak for us in grace and mercy. Confession acknowledges our frail and weak state before God, pleads the blood of Jesus Christ, and reveals genuine, humble and broken hearts. In confession and repentance, God releases pardon, grace and mercy.

sessions.title: Prayer Stage 4: Petitions - Activate the Name of Jesus
sessions.comment: The fourth stage of prayer is to make petitions, requests and needs known to God through Jesus Christ. Take time to make petitions for self, family, church, community, city and nation. Ask for needs, desires, wants and everything on your heart. Ask for impossible things in faith. God loves boldness in asking, and boldness adds fuel to the fire of prayer.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

summary: Iyar is the month for spiritual warfare and establishing proper order of prayer. Territorial dominion requires sustained prayer firepower at home, in prayer groups, with the church, and through prophetic action.
fullNotes: Iyar is the 2nd Bible month. It is the month for spiritual warfare. It is a month for establishing proper order of prayer for everyone in the camp of God.

Every baptized believer, 20 years and above, must commit to effective, fervent and sustained praying. Achieving spiritual dominion in your territory is not an easy matter. Armies win battles because of sustained firepower against the enemy. Church armies must produce sustained prayer firepower.

sessions.title: Praying Alone - Increase Holy Ghost Power at Home Altar
sessions.comment: The first firepower in prayer is developed at the home altar. Every morning and every day, one must pray alone at their personal altar. This pattern fills the individual with Holy Ghost power if sustained. You must learn to pray alone for at least 1-2 hours every day. Your inner altar must have fire every day.

sessions.title: Pray With Your Camp - Presence of the Lord
sessions.comment: The next step of increasing prayer firepower is praying in partnership of twos and threes based on army prayer groups such as apostolic groups, elders groups, deacons and workers groups. This type of praying brings the presence of the Lord to tarry among us.

sessions.title: Pray With the Church - Manifestations of Angels
sessions.comment: The third level of praying that increases prayer firepower exponentially is church joint prayer. Everyone comes for joint prayer. This kind of praying activates angelic manifestations and operations by the Lord, transforms atmospheres, and injects spiritual power in those praying to exercise territorial dominion.

sessions.title: Exercise Prophetic Territorial Dominion With Your Mouth and Hands
sessions.comment: If believers exercise and sustain these three levels of praying, each one becomes a container of immense prayer firepower. It is time to release spiritual prophetic firepower in your territory. Prophesy as you touch things. Speak, command, pronounce, declare, cast out and perform prophetic actions in the name of Jesus.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

summary: Iyar is a season to regroup, put on spiritual armour, and organize church armies for prayer. Every believer should belong to at least one intercessory army group.
fullNotes: Iyar is the 2nd Bible month. It is the month for spiritual warfare. It is a season to regroup and put in place strong church armies. It is a season to put on spiritual armour. Our fight and warfare is against spiritual wickedness in high places.

This week we study the various army groups that need to be enabled and functional in the church. You must belong and participate in at least one army group of intercession. The smallest unit of prayer within any church army group should be two or three people praying together frequently.

sessions.title: Apostolic Intercessors
sessions.comment: The first church army group that must be praying daily is the apostles. Bishops and overseers who belong to this group must lead the church with a life of prayer. These leaders must pray daily to cover the saints and the work God has called them to do.

sessions.title: Elder Intercessors
sessions.comment: Elders entrusted to manage congregations must create time to pray together daily in twos and threes. Elders must be full of prayer. Every elder should have at least one or two elder-level prayer partners.

sessions.title: Deacons & Church Worker Intercessors
sessions.comment: Serious prayer is not for apostles and elders only. Deacons and church workers must also team up in twos and threes to pray daily. Their ministries require much prayer for praise and worship, protocol, security, hospitality and instruments to bless the church.

sessions.title: Congregation Intercessors
sessions.comment: The congregation is the general army. Everyone needs to be a prayer warrior. A praying congregation is transformational and walks in dominion. Every believer must have one or two believer-level prayer partners.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

summary: Iyar is a month of order, organization, spiritual arming and godly warfare. Kingdom advancement requires authentic, orderly and godly leadership.
fullNotes: Iyar is the 2nd Bible month. It is the month of order and organization for bringing dominion. It is the month for spiritual arming and spiritual militarization for godly warfare.

This week we study church leadership and order for dominion. Order and spiritual hierarchy are critically essential for kingdom advancement. God works through leadership to bring victories. To benefit from the kingdom of God, believers must operate under authentic, orderly, godly leadership.

sessions.title: The Priesthood & Apostolic Ministry
sessions.comment: In Israel, the leading spiritual authority was the priesthood. In our day, the equivalent to priests are the apostles of Jesus Christ. Apostleship is the highest rank of spiritual order in the church. Apostles carry the vision, maintain correct doctrine, plant churches, and provide identity and supervision.

sessions.title: Eldership Ministry
sessions.comment: The second level of leadership in the church is that of elders. Elders minister with different graces and follow the vision of the apostles under whom they serve. They lead branch congregations and bridge with other elders.

sessions.title: The Levital Ministry - Deacons & Church Workers
sessions.comment: The third level of church order is the Levites. Today these are deacons and church workers. They help apostles and elders carry out their mandate in hospitality, security, protocol, ushering, praise and worship, children, and cell church ministry.

sessions.title: The Congregation Ministry
sessions.comment: The congregation is the objective and consumer of the apostolic, eldership and deacon ministry. To receive from God, the congregation must be subject, obedient and responsive to leadership in love, respect and honour. Only then can there be power and dominion.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

theme: Prosperity in the Land: Secondary Sources of Money & Wealth
summary: This teaching explores secondary sources of money and wealth: trade, manufacturing, services and support industries, and sports and entertainment.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Merchantry & Trade
sessions.comment: There is money and wealth in trading and merchandise. You can do wholesale, brokerage and retail business by trading on primary sources of wealth. You buy from farmers and producers and sell to consumers.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Manufacturing and Craftsmanship
sessions.comment: From primary sources, you can create unique products for sale. From animals, plants, minerals and other resources, manufacturing can produce great wealth. Create small home-based factories to produce simple but unique products people need daily.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Services & Support Industry
sessions.comment: The services and support industry carries lots of money. Identify a sector where people need technical, medical or other services and begin. Be a people's person and solve people's problems.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Sports & Entertainment
sessions.comment: Personal talents in sports and entertainment can generate income. Athletics, soccer, basketball, acting, music and comedy have money. If you have the talent, exploit it.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

theme: Prosperity in the Land: Primary Sources of Money & Wealth
summary: After Passover, God prospers His people in the land through work. This study explores primary sources of wealth: God's hand of favor, livestock farming, crop farming and agroforestry, mining and fishing.
fullNotes: After Passover, the next item on God's agenda is to prosper His people in the land. After God delivered Israel from Egypt, He supplied them in the wilderness. When they crossed the Jordan, manna stopped and the people had to work to prosper. Without work, God cannot prosper anyone.

The objective of this study is to create interest for creating money and wealth from the primary sources that God has freely provided. Primary sources are things God created that have potential to multiply when taken care of.

sessions.title: God's Hand of Favour
sessions.comment: There are rare occasions when God causes prosperity to come miraculously. Such miracles are exceptions rather than the norm. Trust God for supply miracles, but be found doing something with your hands. Your hands could be the door to the big miracle.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Livestock Farming
sessions.comment: Livestock farming has much money to be made. Rearing domestic animals and birds can produce income if well taken care of. With modern methods, only a little space is needed to begin.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Crop Farming & Agroforestry
sessions.comment: Crop farming and agroforestry have money. Whether cereals, vegetables or fruits, food is needed daily. With modern techniques and technology, a small piece of land is a good place to start and grow.

sessions.title: Put Your Hand to Mining & Fishing
sessions.comment: There is mineral wealth under the earth, including precious stones, metals, rare earth metals and other minerals. Seas and rivers also carry wealth through fish and other water creatures. There is no reason not to put our hands to something for God to bless.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

theme: Celebrating Death, Burial & Resurrection of Christ in the Passover Week
summary: This teaching studies Passover week with a focus on Christ Jesus: consecration, the Lord's Table and foot washing, Unleavened Bread, and Firstfruits with the resurrection of Christ.
fullNotes: The Passover week begins on the 14th of Abib and ends on the 21st of Abib as a feast to the Lord. This study looks at the Passover events mainly with a focus on Christ Jesus. Jesus celebrated the last Passover differently from how the Jews celebrated it, so our example and pattern should mirror that of Jesus Christ.

sessions.title: 10th-13th Abib: Three Days of Consecration of Prayer & Fasting
sessions.comment: The Passover lamb was set apart on the 10th to be killed on the 14th. Christ set Himself apart on the 10th of Abib when He entered Jerusalem. There is a biblical pattern of sanctification for three days before meeting with God. Believers should be set apart before meeting at the Lord's Table.

sessions.title: 14th Abib: The Passover Meal, the Lord's Table & Foot Washing Service
sessions.comment: On the 14th of Abib, three things should be done: the Passover meal, partaking of the Lord's Table with unleavened bread and wine, and foot washing. Jesus ate the last supper earlier because He would be on the cross during the day hours of the 14th of Abib.

sessions.title: Feast of Unleavened Bread
sessions.comment: After midnight toward the early morning hours of the 15th of Abib, Israel left Egypt carrying unleavened bread. On our journey of faith, sin represented by leaven must be put away. The seven days of eating unleavened bread represent our life on earth being free from sin.

sessions.title: 18th Abib: Feast of Firstfruits & the Resurrection of Christ
sessions.comment: The crossing of the sea and the Feast of Firstfruits picture the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead on the third day. Jesus was buried before sunset as the 14th of Abib was ending and rose as the first day of the week began.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov

theme: Celebrations of the 10th of Abib, the Day of Firstborns
summary: Abib/Nissan is the first Bible month and a month of deliverance. This teaching studies the 10th of Abib, the setting apart of Jesus Christ as the Passover Lamb, the celebration and redemption of firstborns, and the blessing of non-firstborns to be firstborns.
fullNotes: Welcome to Abib/Nissan, the 1st month of the Bible calendar. This month corresponds to March/April in the Gregorian calendar. Abib is a month of deliverance. God worked great miracles by the hand of Moses in the 12th month, Adar, in Egypt, but Pharaoh refused to let Israel go free.

No miracle, however powerful, could shake Pharaoh. Miracles in themselves cannot shake the kingdom of darkness. Only clean blood can. To finally deliver Israel, holy blood was needed. A blood sacrifice was necessary to shake the gods of Egypt. The blood was that of firstborns. Deliverance is only by the blood of firstborns. But for Israel, God substituted with a lamb. On the 10th of Abib, God commanded a lamb without blemish, a year old, to be set apart for the blood sacrifice instead of the firstborns of Israel.
Let us study the celebrations that ought to be observed on the 10th of Abib.

sessions.title: 1st Service on 10th Abib: Setting Apart of Jesus Christ, the Passover Blood Sacrifice of Firstborn
sessions.comment: On the 10th of Nissan, a lamb representing the firstborn of Israel was set apart to be sacrificed on the 14th. Joshua also crossed the Jordan with Israel into Canaan on the 10th of Abib. On this date, Jesus Christ entered Jerusalem as the Passover Lamb of sacrifice. As believers, we celebrate the setting apart of Jesus Christ, the actual firstborn of God, as the Lamb of redemption.

sessions.title: 2nd Service on 10th Abib: Celebrating Firstborns
sessions.comment: God could not work salvation without the sacrifice of firstborns. Firstborns are important to God and belong to Him. On the 10th of Abib, the Passover lamb standing in for firstborns was set apart. On this date, firstborns young and old should be set apart, blessed and celebrated before God.

sessions.title: 3rd Service on 10th Abib: Redeeming of Biological Firstborns
sessions.comment: To redeem means to buy back from being killed as a sacrifice to God. Since all firstborns belong to God, they are automatically a sacrifice to God. Redeeming them from God spares them from being a sacrifice. Redeeming of firstborns in Israel happened through the Passover lamb, replacement with Levites, and redemption money paid to the priest.

sessions.title: 4th Service on 10th Abib: Blessing of Non-Firstborns to Be Firstborns
sessions.comment: When a biological firstborn is wicked, they can be replaced by another. Abel replaced Cain, Seth later took over as firstborn, Isaac replaced Ishmael, and Jacob replaced Esau. Parents or grandparents can also swap positions or adopt a child and make them firstborn. Through priestly declaration and offering, one can become firstborn by adoption.

author: Dr. Elder Yaakov
